description: Fetch the structure of Sickle Cell Hemoglobin (PDB: 2HBS). Programmatically modify its crystallographic B-factors to reflect a binary hydrophobicity scale: Assign a B-factor of 50.0 to hydrophobic residues (ALA, VAL, ILE, LEU, MET, PHE, TRP, PRO, TYR), and 10.0 to polar/charged residues (all other types). Save this modified structure to '~/PyMOL_Data/structures/2HBS_hydrophobic.pdb'. Then, create a surface representation colored by these new B-factors. Identify the pathological Valine 6 mutation on the beta chains (Chains B and D) and display them as spheres. Render a PNG figure to '~/PyMOL_Data/images/hbs_surface.png'. Finally, write a brief report to '~/PyMOL_Data/hbs_report.txt' identifying the mutated chains, the mutation itself (Valine 6), the key hydrophobic pocket residues on the beta chains that accept Val6 during polymerization (Phe 85 and Leu 88), and the measured intramolecular distance (in Angstroms) between the C-alpha atom of Val6 and the C-alpha atom of Phe85 on Chain B.
